name: toolbar
title: Toolbar
description: A role=toolbar container that groups related controls (buttons, toggles, a separator, maybe an input) into a single tab stop with APG roving tabindex and RTL-aware arrow-key navigation, built on Base UI's Toolbar. Composes the Commons Button (ToolbarButton) and reuses the Commons Separator (ToolbarSeparator); a Commons ToggleGroup nests directly. Horizontal or vertical orientation, a required accessible name enforced by a dev guard, forced-colors safe.
useWhen: You want a single tab stop with arrow-key navigation over a cluster of controls (a text-editor formatting bar, a table action bar)
Grouping buttons, toggles, and a separator that act on the same target
A dense control strip where Tab-per-button would be tedious for keyboard users
avoidWhen: A simple segmented set of buttons with normal Tab order (use a button-group)
A set of mutually related two-state presses on their own (use a toggle-group)
Primary page navigation between views or routes (use a navigation menu or tabs)
A single standalone action (use a button)

keyboard: Tab moves focus into the toolbar and lands on a single item (roving tabindex); Shift+Tab moves away from the whole toolbar
Arrow keys move focus between items — Left/Right on a horizontal toolbar, Up/Down on a vertical one
Arrow navigation is RTL-aware: in dir=rtl, ArrowLeft moves to the next item that sits visually to the left
Focus loops from the last item back to the first by default (loopFocus)
Disabled items that are not focusable-when-disabled are skipped by arrow navigation
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (each composed Button/Toggle keeps its own >=44px target)
notes: The container renders role=toolbar with aria-orientation and MUST be named via aria-label or aria-labelledby; a dev-only guard warns when neither is present.
Roving tabindex makes the whole toolbar one tab stop; arrow keys move focus between items, unlike button-group which keeps a normal Tab stop per button.

Base UI reads direction from its DirectionProvider, not the DOM dir; an explicit dir prop is fed to the provider, otherwise AmbientDirection supplies the resolved DOM direction so arrow-key order matches the visual order in RTL.

ToolbarButton renders the Commons Button so every variant keeps its border, 44px target, and un-clipped focus ring; the ToolbarSeparator reuses the Commons Separator (a border-drawn rule that survives forced-colors mode).
A Commons ToggleGroup nested inside integrates into the toolbar's single tab stop via Base UI's toolbar context.
